raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Making the draft stronger centrally involves sharpening the thesis, contribution claim, and framing of debate as uncertainty quantification.","R1.2":"The delegation implies checking whether the theoretical argument from assumptions to convergence and calibration claims is coherent.","R1.3":"The draft makes specific empirical and theoretical support claims, so strengthening requires aligning those claims with the reported results.","R1.4":"Claims such as first formal analysis and enduring contribution make novelty positioning a central responsibility.","R1.6":"A broad strengthening request includes improving sentence-level force, clarity, concision, and academic register.","RX.1":"Any projection must preserve uncertainty around unstated theorem details, experiments, and prior work context.","RX.2":"The artifact contains strong claims that require explicit guarding against overstatement.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities and not actually revise or evaluate the draft.","RX.4":"Because the draft concerns factuality and deployment-relevant uncertainty, avoiding misleading strengthening is active.","RX.5":"The projection should remain traceable to concrete phrases and claims in the artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: Making the draft stronger centrally involves sharpening the thesis, contribution claim, and framing of debate as uncertainty quantification.
rationale.R1.2: The delegation implies checking whether the theoretical argument from assumptions to convergence and calibration claims is coherent.
rationale.R1.3: The draft makes specific empirical and theoretical support claims, so strengthening requires aligning those claims with the reported results.
rationale.R1.4: Claims such as first formal analysis and enduring contribution make novelty positioning a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.6: A broad strengthening request includes improving sentence-level force, clarity, concision, and academic register.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection must preserve uncertainty around unstated theorem details, experiments, and prior work context.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains strong claims that require explicit guarding against overstatement.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities and not actually revise or evaluate the draft.
rationale.RX.4: Because the draft concerns factuality and deployment-relevant uncertainty, avoiding misleading strengthening is active.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to concrete phrases and claims in the artifact.
